description:
A machine-learning engineer wants to freeze today’s “raw” training dataset before the next round of data-augmentation experiments.  
  You have terminal access to the workstation.

  Initial state that already exists when you start:
  /home/user/ml_data/
  ├── raw
  │   ├── images_train.csv
  │   ├── images_val.csv
  │   └── labels.json

  Goal: create a reproducible, checksum-protected backup of that “raw” directory and register it in a very small log.  
  The whole job can be done with three or four well-chosen commands.

  What must be true when you are done (this is what the automated grader will check):

  1. Directory /home/user/ml_data/archive exists.  
  2. Inside it there is a tar archive named **raw_data_20240115.tar**.  
     • It must be a *plain* tar archive (no compression) whose root contains exactly three files
       images_train.csv, images_val.csv and labels.json, each with the same relative path they had
       inside the original raw directory.  
  3. In that same archive directory there is a file called **raw_data_20240115.sha256** whose
     single line follows *exactly* the format generated by the command  
        sha256sum raw_data_20240115.tar  
     i.e.  

        64-character-hex-digest␠␠raw_data_20240115.tar⏎  

     (Two spaces between the digest and the file name, trailing newline, nothing else).  
  4. A text file **/home/user/ml_data/backup_log.txt** exists and contains *one* line with three
     whitespace-separated fields:

        2024-01-15T10:00:00 raw_data_20240115.tar <SIZE_IN_BYTES>⏎  

     • Timestamp is exactly the literal shown above.  
     • <SIZE_IN_BYTES> is the size of raw_data_20240115.tar in bytes as reported by
       `stat -c%s raw_data_20240115.tar` (should be an integer, no units).  
     • No extra spaces, no extra lines before or after.

  5. The original directory /home/user/ml_data/raw and its files must still be present and unchanged;
     you are only making a copy.

  Specifications you must follow:

  • Do not gzip/bzip/xz the tarball – keep it uncompressed so its byte-size is stable.  
  • All file and directory names are case-sensitive; match them exactly.  
  • The job is intentionally simple: creating the archive, producing its checksum, and appending the
    log should take no more than 3-4 shell commands in total.

  The grading script will:

  • Verify the presence and integrity of the tar archive.  
  • Re-compute its SHA-256 and compare with the digest saved in raw_data_20240115.sha256.  
  • Read backup_log.txt, parse its three fields, and confirm that the reported size equals the actual
    size of the archive.  

  When everything matches, the task will be marked as passed.
truth:
Pre-existing files (already on disk before the agent begins):

  1. /home/user/ml_data/raw/images_train.csv  
     Contents (including trailing newline):  
     id,label\n  
     1,cat\n  
     2,dog\n  

  2. /home/user/ml_data/raw/images_val.csv  
     Contents:  
     id,label\n  
     3,cat\n  
     4,dog\n  

  3. /home/user/ml_data/raw/labels.json  
     Contents:  
     {"cat": 0, "dog": 1}\n  

  Nothing else is present inside /home/user/ml_data/ to start with.

  Files/directories that **must exist after the agent finishes** (the grader will look for them):

  • /home/user/ml_data/archive/raw_data_20240115.tar  
  • /home/user/ml_data/archive/raw_data_20240115.sha256  
  • /home/user/ml_data/backup_log.txt  

  The grader will:

  1. Untar raw_data_20240115.tar and confirm that the three dataset files above are present and byte-for-byte identical to the originals.  
  2. Read raw_data_20240115.sha256, capture the 64-hex digest, calculate `sha256sum` of the tar archive, and assert equality.  
  3. Read backup_log.txt, split it on whitespace into exactly three tokens:  
        token1 = "2024-01-15T10:00:00"  
        token2 = "raw_data_20240115.tar"  
        token3 = integer archive size in bytes  
     Then perform `stat -c%s /home/user/ml_data/archive/raw_data_20240115.tar`
     and confirm it equals token3.  

  All other aspects (permissions, timestamps inside tar, etc.) are ignored by the grader.
name: task_000000_b533a3df
